title: Quiz: Complex Operations
description: Test your mastery of algebraic operations with complex numbers

questions.question: What is the multiplicative inverse of z = 2 + i?
questions.explanation: The multiplicative inverse is z⁻¹ = z*/|z|². For z = 2 + i: |z|² = 4 + 1 = 5, z* = 2 - i. So z⁻¹ = (2 - i)/5.

questions.question: If |z₁| = 3 and |z₂| = 4, what is |z₁ × z₂|?
questions.explanation: The modulus of a product equals the product of moduli: |z₁ × z₂| = |z₁| × |z₂| = 3 × 4 = 12.

questions.question: Which property states that z₁ × (z₂ + z₃) = z₁ × z₂ + z₁ × z₃?
questions.explanation: This is the distributive property - multiplication distributes over addition. This property is crucial when applying quantum gates to superposition states.

questions.question: For any complex number z, we have (z*)* = z (the conjugate of the conjugate equals the original).
questions.explanation: True! If z = a + bi, then z* = a - bi, and (z*)* = a + bi = z. Taking the conjugate twice returns the original number.

questions.question: The modulus of a sum equals the sum of the moduli: |z₁ + z₂| = |z₁| + |z₂|.
questions.explanation: False! The triangle inequality states |z₁ + z₂| ≤ |z₁| + |z₂| (less than or equal, not equal). Equality only holds when z₁ and z₂ point in the same direction.

questions.question: How can you express Re(z) using z and its conjugate z*?
questions.explanation: For z = a + bi: z + z* = (a + bi) + (a - bi) = 2a = 2·Re(z). Therefore Re(z) = (z + z*)/2.

questions.question: Which of the following are true for the conjugate of a product? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: a) True: The conjugate distributes over multiplication. b) True: Same as (a) since multiplication is commutative. c) False: Both factors must be conjugated. d) True: The modulus of a conjugate equals the modulus: |z*| = |z|, so |(z₁z₂)*| = |z₁z₂| = |z₁||z₂|.
